validate.notes: Checks 1-3 pass: Docker build succeeds, NOP test correctly fails (reward=0), Gold test correctly passes (reward=1). Check 4 (Rubric judge) fails with ICR=0.0 because the rubric judge is fundamentally incompatible with this task. The judge uses extract_config_hunks() which only extracts config file changes (matching CONFIG_RE patterns for CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, etc.), but the gold solution only modifies code files (core/util/paths.ts, core/config/ConfigHandler.ts). Since extract_config_hunks filters out code files, the judge always sees 'Agent added: (nothing)' and fails all rules. The rubric rules (from AGENTS.md about running tests after updates and running format/lint before PR) are process conventions that don't apply to a minimal patch-based gold solution. Additionally, npm run lint doesn't exist at the repo root. The gold solution genuinely does not violate these rules - they simply don't apply. The rules are genuinely unreasonable for evaluating a code-only bug fix.
